output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here's What to Do Next
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, what deductions are legal, and how to demand your money back fast.
content_outline: Why Landlords Withhold Security Deposits (And What's Actually Legal)
Tenant Rights 101: Return Timelines and Rules by State
What Your Landlord Can (and Cannot) Deduct From Your Deposit
What to Do When Your Landlord Won't Return Your Deposit: A 3-Step Action Plan
How to Write a Security Deposit Dispute Letter That Gets Results
When to Take Your Landlord to Small Claims Court
key_points: Landlords can typically only deduct for unpaid rent, damages beyond normal wear and tear, or excessive cleaning—anything else is often illegal depending on state law.
Sending a formal demand letter is the fastest, most effective first step to recover your deposit without immediately paying for court costs.
output.cta: Don't let your landlord keep what's yours. Generate a professional, state-specific security deposit demand letter with DepositBack in minutes—and get your money back faster.
output.target_audience: Problem-aware US renters whose landlords are withholding, delaying, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposit and are actively seeking step-by-step legal guidance to recover their money.
